vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on cron.weekly
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/cron.weekly, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0700 /etc/cron.weekly
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then find -H /etc/cron.weekly/ -maxdepth 0 -perm /u+s,g+xwrs,o+xwrt -type d -exec chmod u-s,g-xwrs,o-xwrt {} \; else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Setting /etc/cron.weekly to 0700 is a standard file permission hardening step that does not affect authentication, networking, or critical services. It follows STIG guidelines for securing cron directories.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly addresses the vulnerability by setting the /etc/cron.weekly directory to 0700 permissions. It follows established STIG guidelines and includes a preliminary existence check, ensuring the directory is present before attempting to change permissions. While the plan is functional, a slightly more streamlined approach could be adopted, but the current methodology is sound and effective.
suggested_improvements: Consider checking the exit status of the ls command or verifying existence with test -d "/etc/cron.weekly" to avoid potential errors if the directory is absent. The plan could also be condensed into a single conditional chmod command that applies the permissions only if the directory exists. These adjustments would make the script more robust but are not strictly necessary for remediation.
